result.markdown:
--- title: "05 — Round-trip" subtitle: "Writing then reading is provenance-safe by spec." jupyter: python3 sidebar: start --- The previous pages built up a typed result with a Receipt. This page is where the Receipt crosses the file boundary and survives. The on-disk format is a versioned public contract — not an implementation detail — which is what makes the round-trip a guarantee instead of a hope. ```{mermaid} %%| fig-cap: "The neuroim value lifecycle. Spatial context and provenance are intrinsic at every step — never reconstructed, never carried in a sidecar." flowchart LR A["raw array
+ affine"] --> B["typed object
NeuroVec / NeuroVol"] B --> C["operation
series_roi · temporal_snr · searchlight_apply"] C --> D["typed result
+ Receipt (.provenance)"] D --> E["file
.nii.gz + neuroim/receipt/v1: extension"] E --> F["typed object again
read_image → .provenance rehydrated"] F -. "audit / chain / reduce" .-> C ``` The loop is the point: a value that leaves the library as a `.nii.gz` re-enters it with the same Receipt, so the next operation can chain, audit, or reduce against a frame it can verify — not one it has to trust. ## Write ::: {#1feae5b2 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} from pathlib import Path import sys import tempfile sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src")) import nibabel as nib import neuroim as ni repo = Path.cwd().parents[1] if Path.cwd().name == "start" else Path.cwd() bold = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_bold.nii.gz")) mask = ni.read_image(str(repo / "golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_mask.nii.gz"), type="vol") tsnr = bold.temporal_snr(mask=mask) tmp = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()) / "tsnr.nii.gz" nib.save(tsnr.to_nibabel(), str(tmp)) tmp.exists(), tmp.stat().st_size > 0 ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` (True, True) ``` ::: ::: `to_nibabel()` serializes the volume to a `nibabel.Nifti1Image` and embeds the Receipt as a NIfTI header extension with `ecode 6` (the standard `comment` ecode) and content prefix `neuroim/receipt/v1:`. The prefix is the version marker; foreign comment extensions from other tools are left alone on read. ::: {.callout-note} Use `to_nibabel()` + `nib.save()` for receipt-preserving writes. Plain writer helpers can be convenient for simple maps, but this explicit path is the one this tutorial uses because it makes the provenance boundary visible. ::: ## Read ::: {#dd724894 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} roundtrip = ni.read_image(str(tmp)) type(roundtrip).__name__ roundtrip.provenance.method_name roundtrip.provenance.input_space_hash[:12], tsnr.provenance.input_space_hash[:12] roundtrip.provenance.mask_hash[:12], tsnr.provenance.mask_hash[:12] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` ('649ca2f312df', '649ca2f312df') ``` ::: ::: The Receipt comes back. Every field that went in comes out — same `method_name`, same hashes, same `n_voxels`. A collaborator who only has `tsnr.nii.gz` can answer *what produced this, from what input, with what mask, what method?* The hashes mean more than equality. If the source BOLD and mask are still on disk, you can recompute their `input_space_hash` and `mask_hash` and confirm they match the volume's Receipt. This is why the Receipt belongs on the file, not in a sidecar: the image carries enough context for a later reader to check which source image and mask produced it. ## Read it without neuroim The spec is ten lines of `nibabel`: ```python import json import nibabel as nib def read_neuroim_receipt(path): img = nib.load(str(path)) for ext in getattr(img.header, "extensions", []) or []: if int(ext.get_code()) != 6: continue text = bytes(ext.get_content()).rstrip(b"\x00").decode("utf-8", "replace") if text.startswith("neuroim/receipt/v1:"): return json.loads(text[len("neuroim/receipt/v1:"):]) return None ``` That snippet does not import `neuroim`. The Receipt is a *public* artifact. A pipeline written in a different toolchain can produce Receipt extensions that `neuroim.read_image` will rehydrate, and a pipeline written in neuroim can be inspected by any tool that reads NIfTI extensions. The full schema and a symmetric reference writer live in [the Receipt spec](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html). ## Refuses to - **Drop the Receipt on `to_nibabel`.** The receipt extension is always embedded when `.provenance` is set. The regression test at [`tests/test_receipt_spec_external_reader.py`](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/tests/test_receipt_spec_external_reader.py) re-reads the file with the ten-line snippet above. - **Confuse a foreign `comment` extension for a Receipt.** Only extensions whose payload starts with the literal `neuroim/receipt/v1:` are treated as Receipts on read. - **Silently accept a future v2 file as v1.** Readers that know only v1 ignore unknown prefixes without raising; they do *not* try to coerce. ## What this finishes You have now read every contract layer the rest of the docs build on: 1. `read_image` returns a typed object. 2. `NeuroSpace` is the spatial frame; `compatible_with` is the alignment check. 3. ROI extraction returns a `Result` with `.values`, `.coords`, `.space`, `.provenance`. 4. Derived maps are volumes that *also* carry `.provenance`. 5. The Receipt survives the file boundary, by a public spec. Everything else in the docs — the cookbook recipes, the searchlight infrastructure, the atlas layer, the alternative voxel-series backends — composes these five pieces. Pick a destination: - **[Concepts](../concepts/space.qmd)** for short-form vocabulary entries. - **[Reference](../reference/index.qmd)** for the generated API. - **[Receipt spec](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html)** for the on-disk format. ## See also - [Concept: Provenance](../concepts/provenance.qmd) — what's rehydrated, what isn't. - [Spec: Receipt v1](../spec/receipt-nifti-extension.html) — the on-disk contract. - [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd) — runnable checks for the IO boundary and provenance round trips.
